working_on: Engine apparatus: close routine close-out gaps. (1) Parent main FF post-push in routine_lifecycle_push.py — fixes the 'every new-worktree boot needs FF' pattern that the user surfaced from this conversation's own boot-freshness check. Routine close pushes from inside its worktree on a feature branch (HEAD:main); origin/main advances but parent's local main does not, so newly-created worktrees inherit stale main. Interactive close already does parent-side FF before push; routine close does not. (2) Auto-sweep current worktree at routine close — Issue #16 follow-on, currently untracked. (3) One-shot manual cleanup of accumulated stale worktrees (Issue #16). (4) S-0071 archive missing_rigor_score 289 → 316 (Issue #25). Boot note: this conversation booted while S-0071 was actively running in funny-roentgen worktree (the user named that explicitly); S-0071 closed normally during plan-mode investigation. The recovery push attempted at /start-engine boot returned 'Everything up-to-date' — origin/main already had S-0071's close.
